segments.text: First listen and then answer the question.

translation.text: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: What is the special importance of a telescope in space?

translation.text: 太空望远镜有什么特殊意义？

segments.text: The Hubble telescope was launched into space by NASA on April 20, 1990 at a cost of over a billion dollars.

translation.text: 1990年4月20日，美国国家航空航天局耗资十多亿美元，把哈勃望远镜发射到太空。

segments.text: Right from the start, there was trouble with the Hubble.

translation.text: 哈勃从一开始就出了问题。

segments.text: The pictures it sent us were very disappointing because its main mirror was faulty.

translation.text: 它传回的照片令人非常失望，因为主镜有缺陷。

segments.text: NASA is now going to put the telescope right, so it will soon be sending up four astronauts to repair it.

translation.text: 美国国家航空航天局现在准备修好望远镜，因此很快会把四名宇航员送上太空进行维修。

segments.text: The shuttle Endeavor will be taking the astronauts to the Hubble.

translation.text: “奋进号”航天飞机将把宇航员送到哈勃望远镜旁。

segments.text: A robot arm from the Endeavor will grab the telescope and hold it while the astronauts make the necessary repairs.

translation.text: “奋进号”上的机械臂将抓住望远镜并固定住，供宇航员完成必要的维修。

segments.text: Of course, the Hubble is above the Earth's atmosphere, so it will soon be sending us the clearest pictures of the stars and distant galaxies that we have ever seen.

translation.text: 当然，哈勃位于地球大气层之外，因此很快会向我们传回有史以来最清晰的恒星和遥远星系照片。

segments.text: The Hubble will tell us a great deal about the age and size of the universe.

translation.text: 哈勃将告诉我们许多有关宇宙年龄和大小的信息。

segments.text: By the time you read this, the Hubble's eagle eye will have sent us thousands and thousands of wonderful pictures.

translation.text: 等你读到本文时，哈勃锐利的“鹰眼”将已经向我们传回成千上万张精彩照片。
